description_long: This is a large, cylindrical red metal barrel with a weathered and slightly rusted surface, featuring two prominent horizontal reinforcement bands around its body. The top is flat with a slightly raised circular section in the center, suggesting a sealed lid. On one side, there is a yellow caution sign with a kangaroo silhouette and the text 'KANGAROO CROSSING NEXT 48 km,' indicating it may be used in outdoor or roadside environments. The barrel's surface shows signs of wear, with patches of chipped paint and minor corrosion, giving it an industrial and utilitarian appearance. There are no visible handles, but the reinforced bands provide structural strength. The overall construction suggests it is designed to store or transport liquids or materials securely.
description: A large, weathered red metal barrel with reinforcement bands and a yellow kangaroo caution sign, showing signs of outdoor use and corrosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellow 'KANGAROO CROSSING' caution sign and the most detailed surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view with reinforcement bands visible and partial view of the caution sign at the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible markings or labels, showing the plain, worn red surface and reinforcement bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with reinforcement bands and a partial glimpse of the caution sign, surface wear visible.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: yellow caution sign with kangaroo and text
